attempted_route: Applied round-1 fix: np.load().transpose(1,0,2) + shape assertions (16540,17,200)/(200,17,200). Built channel-wise U-Net with 4x MaxPool1d(2) encoder + Upsample(scale_factor=2) decoder + skip-add. Adam lr=1e-3, MSELoss, 30 epochs, batch 64.
observation: Crashed after ~1.6s during first training batch at decoder skip-add: RuntimeError tensor size 24 vs 25 at dim 2. MaxPool1d floor-divides 25->12; Upsample(scale_factor=2) returns 24, not 25.
rationale: Timepoints=200 is not divisible by 2^4=16, causing asymmetric pool/upsample: 200->100->50->25->12 then 12->24 (not 25). Skip tensor s4 has length 25 but up4(b) output has length 24; nn.Upsample(scale_factor=2) uses floor so the mismatch is deterministic.
recommended_alternative: Replace nn.MaxPool1d(2) with nn.MaxPool1d(2, stride=2) and replace nn.Upsample(scale_factor=2) with nn.ConvTranspose1d(C, C, kernel_size=2, stride=2, groups=C) to guarantee exact length doubling; alternatively use only 3 pool stages (200->100->50->25->12 skipped) so T=200 stays divisible at all levels.
